_meta.note: Built to the Armagh-locked standard. Longford lost the Leinster opener heavily to Westmeath, then in the Tailteann Cup lost to Fermanagh, beat Clare, and lost to Down in Round 3. They reached the Division 4 league final (lost to Carlow after extra time) and won promotion to Division 3. Four championship games.
_meta.verification: All scorelines, venues and dates verified against primary tables and reports (RTE, gaa.ie, Irish Times, The42). One verified discrepancy on the Round 3 scoreline is flagged. Per-player championship scorer detail is partial.

manager.name: Mike Solan
manager.note: Second season. A Mayo man who guided Mayo to All-Ireland Under-21 glory in 2016 and previously served as Leitrim assistant to Andy Moran; took over Longford from Paddy Christie in September 2024 on a three-year term. Reached a Division 4 league final and a Tailteann Round 3 in 2026.

league.note: Reached the Division 4 league final at Croke Park, losing to Carlow after extra time (Carlow 1-24, Longford 2-18). Longford forced extra time through Matthew Carey having trailed, with goals from Oisín O'Toole and Oran Kenny, but a Ronan Bleakley red card in extra time turned it Carlow's way. Promoted to Division 3 alongside Carlow.

source.snippet: Leading by five points with 14 minutes of normal time to go, Joe Murphy's side was reeled in by a determined Longford who forced extra time at 2-14 to 1-17... Carlow 1-24 Longford 2-18.
Down got their Tailteann Cup campaign back on track as they rebounded from defeat in Offaly to post a four-goal victory away to Longford.
Round 1 Longford 1-16 5-25 Westmeath Glennon Brothers Pearse Park

results.note: A 21-point home thrashing by an in-form Westmeath (the widest margin and most goals of the Leinster championship), which sent Longford into the Tailteann Cup. Full scorer list not captured. Westmeath's perspective is held in Westmeath's file. (Leinster terms this the preliminary round / Round 1.)

source.snippet: First Round 10 May Fermanagh 1-24 2-17 Longford Brewster Park

results.note: A four-point Tailteann opening loss away to Fermanagh, dropping Longford into the Round 2B knockout. Full scorer list not captured. Fermanagh's perspective is held in Fermanagh's file.

scorers.note: man of the match; 1 two-pointer, 2 frees; late go-ahead goal

source.snippet: Longford reached the knockout stage as a Round 2B winner over Clare.

results.note: A two-point knockout win away in Ennis that kept Longford alive and eliminated Clare. The two scorer tallies shown (Dylan Farrell 1-7, Oran Kenny 1-4) are carried from earlier research and were NOT re-sourced in this pass, so treat as provisional pending verification against a primary report. Clare's perspective is held in Clare's file.

scorers.note: substitute; 62nd-min goal from a Cian Brady pass; 1 two-pointer

source.snippet: they led 4-13 to 0-9... Longford battled on with substitute Dessie Reynolds registering green and orange flags to leave it 4-18 to 1-15 at the full-time whistle.

results.note: Elimination game: a heavy home loss to tournament favourites Down, who led 2-8 to 0-7 at half-time and 4-13 to 0-9 before a late Longford rally. SCORELINE DISCREPANCY: The42's match report gives the full-time score as Down 4-18 Longford 1-15 (a 12-point margin, internally consistent with its running score), while the RTE results table lists Longford 1-14. Down's 4-18 is agreed by both; Longford's total is recorded here as 1-15 per the detailed match report, with the RTE table's 1-14 noted as the alternative. Margin therefore 12 or 13 points. Partial scorer list. Down's perspective will live in Down's file (Down still alive, Tailteann finalist).

collection_meta.collected_by: Claude (research session, not yet hand-verified by Rory)
fields_not_found: Full Longford championship scorer lists (only partial detail for the Clare R2B win, carried provisionally from earlier research, and the Down R3 loss)
Resolution of the Round 3 Longford scoreline (1-15 per The42 match report vs 1-14 per RTE results table)
collection_meta.notes: Longford is a light file with a respectable spring (a Division 4 league final at Croke Park, lost to Carlow after extra time, and promotion to Division 3) but a thin championship: a 21-point Leinster hammering by Westmeath, then a single Tailteann win over Clare before Down ended them in Round 3. Dylan Farrell, Oran Kenny and wing-back Dessie Reynolds (a prolific two-point shooter in the league final) are the names. Two flags for the verification pass: re-source the Clare R2B scorer tallies, and resolve the one-point discrepancy on the Down Round 3 scoreline.
